name: validation
context: Validate, constrain, and refine data with Zod's built-in validators, custom refinements, and error handling
info: This skill covers everything about validating data with Zod: built-in validators for strings (.min, .max, .length, .email, .url, .uuid, .regex, .startsWith, .endsWith, .trim, .toLowerCase, .toUpperCase, .datetime, .ip, .cuid, .cuid2, .ulid, .includes, .emoji), numbers (.gt, .gte, .lt, .lte, .int, .positive, .nonnegative, .negative, .nonpositive, .multipleOf, .finite, .safe), dates (.min, .max), and arrays (.nonempty, .min, .max, .length). Custom validation via .refine() for simple checks and .superRefine() for full control over error issues. Error handling with ZodError, .flatten(), .format(), custom error messages, error maps, and path tracking. Parsing modes: .parse() (throws), .safeParse() (returns result), and async variants. See [[zod/schemas]] for defining shapes and [[zod/transform]] for data transformation.

sections.title: Parsing modes
sections.body: `.parse(data)` validates and returns typed data, throwing `ZodError` on failure. `.safeParse(data)` returns `{ success: true, data }` or `{ success: false, error }` without throwing — preferred for user-facing validation. Async variants `.parseAsync()` and `.safeParseAsync()` are required when schemas contain async refinements or transforms. All methods strip unknown keys by default (configurable via `.passthrough()` or `.strict()` on [[zod/schemas]] objects).

sections.title: String validators
sections.body: Zod provides built-in string validators: `.min(n)` / `.max(n)` / `.length(n)` for length, `.email()` / `.url()` / `.uuid()` / `.cuid()` / `.cuid2()` / `.ulid()` for format, `.regex(re)` for pattern matching, `.includes(str)` / `.startsWith(str)` / `.endsWith(str)` for substring checks, `.datetime()` for ISO 8601, `.ip()` for IP addresses, `.emoji()` for emoji-only. Transform methods: `.trim()`, `.toLowerCase()`, `.toUpperCase()`. All accept an optional custom error message as the last argument.

sections.title: Number validators
sections.body: Number constraints: `.gt(n)` / `.gte(n)` (alias `.min(n)`), `.lt(n)` / `.lte(n)` (alias `.max(n)`), `.int()`, `.positive()` / `.nonnegative()` / `.negative()` / `.nonpositive()`, `.multipleOf(n)` (alias `.step(n)`), `.finite()`, `.safe()`. Each accepts an optional custom error message. Combine freely: `z.number().int().positive().lte(100)` validates a positive integer up to 100.

sections.title: Custom refinements with .refine() and .superRefine()
sections.body: `.refine(validator, options)` adds a custom validation check. The validator function receives the parsed value and returns a boolean (or Promise<boolean> for async). Options: `{ message, path, params }`. Chain multiple `.refine()` calls for multiple checks. `.superRefine((val, ctx) => { ... })` gives full control — call `ctx.addIssue()` to add custom errors with specific codes, paths, and messages. Use `.superRefine()` for cross-field validation on objects, like password confirmation matching.

sections.title: Error handling and formatting
sections.body: Validation failures produce a `ZodError` instance with `.issues` (array of ZodIssue objects). Each issue has `code`, `path`, `message`, and type-specific fields. Format errors with `.flatten()` for flat `{ formErrors, fieldErrors }` objects (great for form UIs), or `.format()` for nested objects matching the schema shape. Customize messages inline (`z.string().min(1, "Required")`), at the type level (`z.string({ required_error: "..." })`), or globally via `z.setErrorMap()`. Issue codes include `invalid_type`, `too_small`, `too_big`, `invalid_string`, `custom`, and more.

scripts.name: Validate Data Against Schema
scripts.description: Parse and validate JSON data against a Zod schema, returning typed data or formatted errors

schema.description: Zod schema definition as TypeScript code string

data.description: The data to validate (any JSON-serializable value)

mode.description: Parsing mode — parse throws on error, safeParse returns result

depends.reason: Zod must be installed for validation
A schema must be defined before validation
